title:
Module 5
Phone Setup / Warmup
subtitle: Social Media Setup for Your Phone and Warmup for Your Social Media Accounts

blocks.content: Your device setup is the foundation of account safety. Platforms track device IDs, IP addresses, and usage patterns. If you run multiple AI personas from the same phone on the same network, you are asking to get flagged. This lesson covers the correct way to set up your devices before creating a single account.

items: One dedicated phone per persona (ideal setup)
If sharing a device: max 2 accounts per phone (risky but workable)
Use separate browser profiles or app clones if needed
Never log into two personas on the same app simultaneously
Factory reset any used phone before starting a new persona
Used phones work perfectly — you do not need the latest model
Use mobile data (4G/5G) during warmup — not WiFi
If using WiFi: use a VPN set to a US location
Each persona should ideally have a different IP footprint
Never use public WiFi for account activity
Keep your VPN location consistent — do not jump between cities or countries

left.content: Not all VPNs are equal. The type of IP address matters more than the VPN brand.

items: Residential proxies — Best option. IPs look like real home connections. Platforms cannot easily detect them
Mobile proxies — Excellent option. IPs look like real phone connections. Very hard to flag
Datacenter VPNs (NordVPN, ExpressVPN, etc.) — Workable but riskier. Datacenter IPs are easier for platforms to flag
For starting out, a quality VPN like NordVPN or Surfshark set to a US city is sufficient
For scaling to multiple accounts, invest in residential or mobile proxies
Each account needs a unique phone number for verification
Prepaid SIM cards work perfectly and are cheapest ($10-$30)
Virtual numbers (Google Voice, TextNow) are riskier — platforms sometimes flag these
eSIMs are a convenient option for managing multiple numbers on one device
Never share a phone number across multiple personas
Keep a record of which SIM/number belongs to which persona
Instagram
Threads (linked to Instagram)
TikTok
Telegram
VPN app
Photo gallery app for content storage
Notes app for captions and prompt templates

right.content: For operators scaling beyond their first persona, dedicated phones become expensive. Antidetect browsers and cloud phones let you manage multiple accounts from one machine with isolated fingerprints.

items: GoLogin — Manages thousands of browser profiles, each with unique fingerprints. Low cost, good for scaling. Has a native Android app
GeeLark — Cloud-based Android environments for Instagram warmup. Simulates real device behavior with gradual engagement
Multilogin — Enterprise-grade, each cloud phone gets unique IMEI, Android ID, MAC address
These tools are NOT needed for your first persona — start with a physical phone
Add antidetect tools when you scale to 3+ accounts and need to manage them efficiently

blocks.content: Account creation is not just signing up. Every decision you make in the first 24 hours sends signals to the platform's algorithm. This lesson covers how to create accounts that look human from the very first second.

items: Human-sounding name — first name or first name + initial
No random numbers (e.g., emma38472 is a red flag)
No sexual or suggestive words anywhere in the username
Easy to remember and search after seeing it once
Same username across all platforms for this persona
Check availability on Instagram, TikTok, Threads, and Twitter before committing

left.content:
Username: emma.laine / lilyy.rose / sofiamrr
Real-sounding, memorable, no red flags. Could be a real person's handle.

Username: hotgirl_2024 / xXsexyEmmaXx / model.promo.page
Instant red flags for platforms and audiences. Screams fake or promotional.

items: Create a unique email for each persona (Gmail works fine)
Email should match the persona name (e.g., emmalaine.personal@gmail.com)
Never use the same email across multiple personas
Enable 2FA on every account from day one — protects against account theft
Add a profile photo (clear face shot of your AI model)
Write a short, casual bio — personality-driven, not salesy
No links in bio during warmup — add after 3+ weeks
No selling language, no emoji spam, no hashtag stuffing
Set account to public
Do NOT add 'DM for collabs' or 'link below' type language

right.content:
Bio: '22 dreaming in california'
Simple, human, personality-driven. Could be any real girl's bio.

Bio: 'DM for exclusive content. Link below.'
Screams promotional account. Will get flagged immediately.

items: Instagram: Create account, add photo and bio within first day, start engaging
Threads: Linked to Instagram — set up after Instagram is created
Twitter/X: Text-only posts first, no images for 3 days
Reddit: Lurk mode for first 2 days — no posts, no comments

blocks.title: TikTok Exception
blocks.content: On TikTok, do NOT add username, bio, or profile picture immediately after account creation. Close the app for 20 minutes after creating the account. Then come back and add details slowly over the first 2-3 days. TikTok is the most sensitive to early behavior.

The warmup phase is the most important phase of your entire account lifecycle. It exists to teach the algorithm that your account is a real human. If you rush this, everything after breaks. There are no shortcuts. The platforms are watching your first 21 days more closely than any other period.
Duration: 21-28 days for Instagram/Threads. 9 days for TikTok. 14 days for Twitter/X. 7 days for Reddit. This phase is boring on purpose. Boring means safe.

items: Scroll for 15-30 minutes daily
Like posts from English-speaking creators (10-15 per session)
Watch Reels and Stories naturally
Follow 5-10 accounts per day maximum
No posting, no comments yet
First carousel posts (2-3 per week)
Face-focused, lifestyle, clean SFW content only
Start Stories (2-3 per week) — selfies, food, daily life
Comment occasionally on other posts (normal, human comments)
No questions, no CTA, no suggestive captions
Increase to 3-4 carousels per week
Daily Stories (selfies, behind-the-scenes, personality content)
Start Threads posts if engagement is stable
Increase commenting activity (still natural, not promotional)
Add link in bio at end of Week 3 (if all signals are green)
Begin Reels posting (start with 1 per day)
Full Story cadence (3-5 per day)
Threads posts scaling to 3-4 per day
Engagement should feel natural and consistent by now
Day 1: Create account with no username/bio/picture. Close app 20 min. Browse FYP 30-60 min. No likes
Day 2: Browse FYP 30 min. No follows, no posts, no likes
Day 3: Browse FYP 30 min. Like 3-5 videos. No follows, no posts
Day 4: Browse 30-40 min. Like 5-7 videos. Write 1 comment. No follows, no posts
Day 5: Add profile picture, bio, username (manually). Browse 30-40 min. Like 8-10 videos. 2 comments
Day 6: Browse 30-40 min. Like 8-10 videos. 2 comments. No posting yet
Day 7: Browse 30-40 min. Like 10 videos. 3 comments. Post your first TikTok
Days 8-9: Browse 30-40 min. Like 8-10 videos. 2-3 comments. Post second and third TikToks. Activate analytics
After Day 9: Warmup complete. Begin normal posting schedule
Days 1-3: 1 text post per day, 10-20 likes, 5 replies. No images
Days 4-7: 1-2 SFW posts per day, 30 likes, 10 replies. Start mixing text and images
Days 8-14: 2-3 posts per day, 20 replies. Add images and short threads
After Day 14: Check shadowban status. If all green, begin full posting. Add link in bio

blocks.title: Critical Rule
blocks.content: If you rush the warmup, everything after breaks. There are no shortcuts. The platforms are watching your first 21 days more closely than any other period. Behave like a normal person, not a marketer. The warmup is boring because boring is safe.

sections.header: Lesson 4: Warmup Mistakes That Get You Banned

blocks.content: Most account bans happen during the warmup phase because people cannot resist skipping steps. This lesson covers every common mistake and why each one triggers platform flags. Study these carefully — every mistake listed here is one that real operators have lost accounts to.

items: Posting content on Day 1 (the platform has no trust in you yet)
Adding a link in bio during warmup (screams promotional account)
Following 50+ accounts per day (bot behavior trigger)
Liking hundreds of posts in a session (automated pattern detection)
Using the same caption style on every post (template detection)
Posting suggestive content before the account has trust built
Using banned or spammy hashtags during warmup
Copy-pasting captions (platforms detect identical text patterns)
Using third-party automation tools that require account login
Switching between multiple accounts rapidly on the same device

left.content: Instagram has invisible rate limits. Exceeding these triggers automatic flags even on established accounts. During warmup, stay well below these numbers.

items: Maximum 150 likes per hour (stay under 50 during warmup)
Maximum 60 comments per hour (stay under 10 during warmup)
Maximum 60 follows/unfollows per hour (stay under 10 during warmup)
These limits are per hour, not per session — pace yourself throughout the day

left.title: The Invisible Flag
left.content: You will NOT get a warning. Platforms silently reduce your reach. You will not know you are shadowbanned until weeks later when nothing grows. Prevention is the only cure. There is no undo button.

items: Wait 3-5 days before first post on any platform
Keep all warmup content clean, casual, and unremarkable
Engage like a real user: varied comments, natural timing, genuine interest
Add link in bio only after Week 3 when engagement is stable
Keep follow/unfollow activity low and spaced throughout the day
Post slowly and increase frequency gradually over weeks
Let the algorithm come to you — do not force it
Type every caption and comment manually — never copy-paste

right.content:
Week 1: Scroll, like 10-15 posts, follow 5 accounts. No posts.
Week 2: First carousel, 2 stories. Light commenting.
Week 3: 3 carousels, daily stories, start Threads.
Week 4: Add link in bio, start Reels.

Result: Healthy account with organic growth signals.

Day 1: 5 posts, 100 follows, link in bio, hashtag spam.
Day 2: Suggestive content, 200 likes in one session.
Day 3: 'Why isn't my account growing?'
Day 5: Shadowbanned. Account is effectively dead.

Result: Wasted time, wasted content, start over from scratch.

The warmup phase is a filter. The operators who succeed are the ones who can sit through 3-4 weeks of boring, slow, deliberate activity without breaking protocol. If you cannot handle the boredom of warmup, you will not handle the grind of growth. This phase tests your discipline more than your skills.

right.title: Recovery Cost
right.content: If you rush warmup and get banned: you lose all content posted, all followers gained, all warmup time invested, and you must start over with a new device, new SIM, new IP, new account. The cost of impatience is measured in weeks, not minutes.

summary_points: One dedicated phone per persona — never share devices or IP addresses between personas
Use residential or mobile proxies for best safety — datacenter VPNs are workable but riskier
Antidetect browsers (GoLogin) and cloud phones (GeeLark) are for scaling beyond your first persona — not needed to start
Usernames must be human-sounding, memorable, and consistent across all platforms
No links, no selling language, and no suggestive content during warmup — period
Instagram warmup: 21-28 days. TikTok: 9 days. Twitter/X: 14 days. No shortcuts exist
Stay well below platform rate limits during warmup: under 50 likes/hour, under 10 follows/hour on Instagram
Behave like a real person: scroll, like, comment naturally before posting content
Most bans happen during warmup because people rush — patience is mandatory and non-negotiable
